title: Cubes, Masonry, Scramble, Springs & Transitions
description: Five live UI pattern demos: 3D scroll cube, CSS masonry, text scramble decode, spring drag, and View Transitions morph.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven 3D Cube Gallery
drops.description: Horizontal card gallery that rotates in 3D perspective as you scroll — hover to tilt, click to spin 360°.
drops.concept: Cards sit in a horizontal scroll container and rotate on the Y-axis based on their distance from center. Each card is a real CSS 3D cube with front, back, and side faces. Hover adds fine-grained mouse-tilt and a full spin fires on click. Inspired by Codrops' scroll-driven 3D cube tutorial (May 26, 2026).
drops.source: Codrops — Building a Scroll-Driven 3D Cube Gallery (May 2026)

interaction.mouse: Hover to tilt the card in 3D

drops.title: CSS Masonry Layout
drops.description: Native grid-template-rows: masonry (Interop 2026) with JS column-count fallback and container queries.
drops.concept: Demonstrates the native CSS masonry layout proposal that is a key focus area of Interop 2026. Cards of variable height pack into columns with no JavaScript measurement. A JS column-count fallback ensures graceful degradation. A secondary demo shows container queries switching column counts based on container width, not viewport.
drops.source: Interop 2026 / W3C CSS Grid Level 3 Masonry Proposal

interaction.mouse: Hover cards for cursor-tracked shimmer glow
interaction.click: + Add Card button dynamically inserts new variable-height cards

drops.title: Text Scramble Decode
drops.description: Characters resolve from random noise into words on scroll into view — type your own text to scramble.
drops.concept: An Intersection Observer triggers a per-character scramble animation as headings enter the viewport. Each character flickers through random glyphs from a set (A-Z, 0-9, symbols) before locking onto its target. The hero word loops through a word list. An interactive input lets visitors type any text and watch it scramble and decode. Speed is tunable.
drops.source: Classic CodePen scramble technique, modernised with Intersection Observer and requestAnimationFrame (2026)

interaction.mouse: Scroll to trigger each heading's decode
interaction.click: Type custom text and click Scramble

drops.title: Spring Rubber-Band Drag
drops.description: Draggable cards with iOS-style elastic resistance and spring-back physics — tune stiffness, damping, and mass.
drops.concept: Each card is draggable with resistance that increases non-linearly with distance from origin, mimicking iOS rubber-banding. On release, a spring integrator (F = -kx - dv) animates the card back with tunable overshoot. Cards can be dropped onto snap zones. Trail lines and spring-link indicators show the elastic force visually. Physics panel exposes stiffness, damping, and mass.
drops.source: iOS UIKit spring animation model / WWDC physics animation talks

interaction.mouse: Drag to stretch, release to spring back
interaction.touch: Touch drag supported with pointer events

drops.title: View Transitions Morph
drops.description: Click a card to morph it into a detail view with shared-element transitions — pure document.startViewTransition().
drops.concept: Demonstrates the View Transitions API (Baseline 2025) for same-document shared-element morphing. Clicking a card assigns view-transition-name to its image, title, and badge, then calls document.startViewTransition() to swap to the detail view. The browser automatically captures snapshots and interpolates position, size, and shape. A support badge shows native vs fallback mode.
drops.source: Chrome Developers — View Transitions API guide (web.dev 2025)

interaction.mouse: Click any card to morph to detail
interaction.click: Back button returns with reverse transition
interaction.touch: Tap cards to open detail view
